design_philosophy.core_loop: Select squad positioning and movement with mouse, direct fire at enemies, manage limited explosive ammo, progress through mission objectives across procedurally-varied terrain
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Squad fragility and outnumbering vs. resource scarcity (limited grenades/rockets); tactical positioning required to survive despite superior enemy numbers
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a small but elite military squad through varied combat scenarios, making tactical decisions under pressure
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Darkly humorous anti-war satirical tone; deliberately fragile soldiers and expendable squad mechanics reflect war's human cost; point-and-click strategy controls grafted onto action gameplay is unusual and intentional, rejecting arcade conventions for methodical tactics

difficulty.lives_system: Squad-based: individual soldiers are one-hit kills; mission failure if entire squad eliminated
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpointing; 24 missions divided into 72 levels total
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: player can split squad into smaller units for risk mitigation, choose to shoot ammo crates for splash damage vs. retrieving them, carefully position troops to avoid wasting resources

progression.upgrade_system: No persistent upgrades; soldiers spawn with baseline machine guns; secondary weapons (grenades, rockets) provided as mission pickups in supply crates
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential mission unlock through level completion; enemy infantry becomes progressively more powerful across 24 missions
replayability_hooks: Varied terrain types (jungle, snow, desert) with unique environmental hazards (rivers, quicksand, mines, booby traps)
Squad positioning puzzles encourage different tactical approaches
Unique vehicles per terrain (snowmobiles in snow levels)
Resource optimization challenges across repeatable missions

world_building_techniques: Military-themed scenario design with varied geographic settings
Darkly humorous tone in presentation conveys satirical anti-war message
Enemy building destruction and spawn mechanics reinforce war scenario
Rural setting inspiration (Emmerdale Farm) grounds levels in recognizable environments

faction_indicators: Player-controlled squad vs. enemy forces with escalating strength
danger_signals: Vehicles and turrets represent escalating threat tiers
Terrain hazards (quicksand, mines) punish careless movement
environmental_storytelling: Enemy buildings spawn infantry, establishing supply chain mechanics
Varied terrain (jungle, snow, desert) conveys geographic mission diversity
Vehicle types match environment (snowmobiles in snow)
Destructible obstacles and hazards create tactical decision points
